item.title: KKR eyes multibillion-dollar sale of data centre cooling company
item.description: Private capital firm seeks big return on investment in CoolIT Systems as AI boom boosts valuations

item.title: Here is the latest.
The guards rise again: the force still shaping Iran
item.description: Should the regime survive, hardliners believe the IRGC’s influence will be ever greater

item.title: A Loose Band of Emerging Powers Is Divided Over Iran
item.description: Brazil, China and Russia all denounced the U.S.-Israeli attacks, but other nations in the BRICS group haven’t, even though Iran is a fellow member.

item.title: Ahead of Colombia’s Elections, Violence on the Campaign Trail Rattles Politicians
item.description: Politicians in Colombia have increasingly become targets of violence. A rise in kidnappings, death threats and assassinations has shaken the country ahead of the vote.

item.title: Trump Executive Order Protected Weedkiller Roundup and a Munition, White Phosphorus
item.description: Citing national security, an unusual executive order gave protection to the herbicide Roundup. It also protected the U.S.’s only supply of a controversial, highly flammable munition.

item.title: ‘It’s our moment’: rival exiles lobby the US to lead Iran after the war
item.description: The son of the ousted shah and a longstanding rebel group spend big in Washington in hopes of ruling in Tehran

item.title: Israel Strikes Oil Facilities in Iran
item.description: Huge flames erupted into the sky over Tehran and a nearby city on Saturday.

item.title: China signals it wants a Trump visit despite Iran war
item.description: Foreign minister Wang Yi says Beijing ‘positive and open’ on this month’s summit between the US and Chinese presidents

item.title: Trump accuses Starmer of seeking to 'join wars after we've already won'
item.description: The remarks come after a public back-and-forth between Trump and Sir Keir Starmer over UK involvement the US's joint offensive with Israel against Iran.

item.title: US embassy in Oslo hit by explosion, Norway police say
item.description: No injuries are reported after the blast in the Norwegian capital in the early hours on Sunday.

item.title: Gen Z-Backed Rapper Is on Course to Lead Nepal With Landslide Win
item.description: Balendra Shah, the onetime rap artist and former mayor of the country’s capital, is on course to become the country’s next prime minister.

item.title: In Russia’s Former Capital, Restoring the Past to Survive the Present
item.description: With political activism off-limits, residents of St. Petersburg are finding purpose and community in the “politics of small deeds,” repairing and cleaning architectural treasures.

item.title: Gulf businesses buy up political violence insurance as conflict spreads
item.description: Data centres, energy projects and hotels move to limit potential losses

item.title: Liberals should be less shy about saying what they stand for
item.description: Failure to defend their ideology has created an intellectual vacuum on the American left

item.title: Mexico drug cartel chief’s ‘narco-ledger’ shows ties to authorities
item.description: Papers allegedly belonging to deceased crime boss Nemesio ‘El Mencho’ Oseguera suggest he bribed police and other organisations

item.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah unseats Nepal's ex-PM as he heads for victory
item.description: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.

item.title: Trump Assembles a New Coalition to ‘Eradicate’ Cartels
item.description: At a gathering in Florida, the president asked the leaders of a dozen Latin American and Caribbean nations to help the U.S. military crush armed trafficking groups.

item.title: Weight-loss treatments boom as Kenyan attitudes to beauty change
item.description: Kenyan celebrities are driving a surge in weight‑loss and cosmetic procedures, sparking criticism and concerns.

item.title: Spectacular images reveal unique sea creatures and corals off Caribbean islands
item.description: Scientists discover underwater mountain ranges, golden towers of coral, and never-before-seen sea creatures.

item.title: The heartwarming tale of a father, a daughter, and a wedding band wowing India
item.description: Band Baaja Bitiya is the moving tale of a father who goes to rescue his daughter from her matrimonial home.

item.title: In War’s First Week, a Punishing Military Campaign With No Coherent Endgame
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have pounded Iran’s leadership and undercut its defense capabilities, but President Trump has offered wildly different explanations for what he hopes to achieve.

item.title: Dancers loved practising in this Singapore walkway. Then the complaints came
item.description: In super-organised Singapore, disputes over public space are a battle between control and chaos.

item.title: UK to charter flight for British nationals out of Dubai
item.description: The flight is due to depart from Dubai early next week for British nationals wanting to leave the region.

item.title: Russia Attacks Kharkiv and Kyiv in Ukraine
item.description: At least 10 people were killed when a Russian missile hit a five-story apartment building in the city of Kharkiv, and Kyiv and several other regions also came under attack.

item.title: Pakistan Praised Trump. Now It Risks Being Caught Up in His War With Iran
item.description: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran have set off a crippling energy crisis and sparked deadly protests in one of the world’s most populous Muslim nations.

item.title: The Iran war puts Vladimir Putin in a tough spot
item.description: Higher oil prices are poor compensation for showing Russia’s weakness

item.title: Defense Without U.S. Help Is a Live Topic for Canada, Japan and Australia
item.description: The leaders of Japan and Canada are making a unified front on defense cooperation as President Trump raises the pressure over military spending.

item.title: Swinney would 'consider' US military plane ban from Prestwick Airport
item.description: The first minister says he wants clarity from the UK government about whether the planes were involved in the US-Israeli action in the Middle East.

item.title: Why did Iran's president apologise?
item.description: The apology may hint at a political repositioning inside Iran, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azmi.

item.title: Heavy rains and flooding kills at least 23 in Nairobi
item.description: Flights in and out of Nairobi Airport have been disrupted while some roads in the capital are submerged.

item.title: US faced with few good options to tamp down surging oil prices
item.description: Experts say reopening Strait of Hormuz remains most viable option as ideas such as influencing futures market fade

item.title: Europe Didn’t Want War With Iran. But So Far, It Can’t Stay Out of It.
item.description: From London to Rome and beyond, leaders are facing diplomatic headwinds and criticism at home as they take part in a conflict they did not seek.

item.title: How Good Intentions Helped Pave Trump’s Road to Iran
item.description: Humanitarians proposed a loophole in international law. Decades later, Trump is jumping through it.

item.title: Russian strike on Kharkiv apartment block kills ten
item.description: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky says other people - including children - are injured as rescuers continue to search through rubble.

item.title: We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said
item.description: Ordinary Iranians reflect on seven days of conflict and where they see their country going next.

item.title: For Xi, Trump’s Embrace of War Proves China Needs More Power
item.description: China is learning lessons from the war in Iran. Chief among them, the United States may pose an even greater threat than Beijing thought.

item.title: Surfers in Munich (Yes, Munich) Just Want Their Wave Back
item.description: For decades, enthusiasts rode the cresting Eisbach creek in the southern German city, some 200 miles from the nearest coast. But the wave has vanished, prompting arguments about how to restore it.

item.title: There's an inflation wave coming. How worried should we be?
item.description: Economic consequences are an intrinsic aspect of the Iran conflict, writes BBC economics editor Faisal Islam.

item.title: Swiss to vote on right-wing push to slash licence fee for public broadcaster
item.description: The move is backed by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, which says the current fee is unjustified because of the high cost of living.

item.title: Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran as Putin speaks with Iran's president
item.description: The president also calls on Tehran to pick new leaders who are "acceptable" to the White House after six days of war.

item.title: China spent years building ties in Latin America. Can Trump make room for the US?
item.description: US President Donald Trump will meet with ideologically-aligned Latin American leaders to try and counter China's influence in the region.

item.title: The final voyage of the Iranian warship sunk by the US
item.description: The sinking of an Iranian ship by the US near Sri Lanka brings the conflict uncomfortably close to India.

item.title: Armed robots take to the battlefield in Ukraine war
item.description: Ukraine has embarked on a programme to deploy armed robots on the battlefield against Russian forces.

item.title: Trump wants to pick Iran's new leader - will a hostile regime under fire agree?
item.description: The president's vision of Iran's future could meet fierce opposition, writes chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet.

item.title: Russian athletes march at Paralympic opening ceremony
item.description: Athletes from Russia and Belarus parade behind their nations' flags at the opening ceremony of the Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics as the 50th anniversary edition of the Games officially gets under way.

item.title: The Iran war has entered a new phase
item.description: Both sides have changed their tactics, our data analysis finds

item.title: Tory peer quits after Lords probe into PPE deals
item.description: It comes after a Lords standards probe into his contacts with ministers and advisers during the pandemic.

item.title: Israel sees a chance to destroy Hizbullah at last
item.description: Iran’s most powerful proxy, though weakened, is still in the fight

item.title: Trump sets his sights on crisis-hit Cuba after Iran action
item.description: The US president has claimed the Cuban authorities “want to make a deal so bad”.

item.title: Parliament refurb chair defends potential £40bn costs
item.description: Simon Thurley says the estimated cost does not seem like a "completely ridiculous figure" to him.

item.title: Mexico to deploy 100,000 security personnel for World Cup
item.description: World Cup co-hosts Mexico plan to deploy nearly 100,000 security personnel to protect fans at this summer's tournament, amid ongoing cartel violence in the country.

item.title: Fatbikes are wreaking havoc in Sydney's wealthy beach suburbs
item.description: Teens are infuriating locals by riding over golf courses and doing wheelies on the Harbour Bridge.

item.title: Ukrainian bank workers released after detention in Hungary
item.description: Kyiv has reacted furiously after officials in Budapest accused staff transporting cash to Ukraine of money laundering.

item.title: Ministers offer Tories more peers to end hereditary bill standoff
item.description: It comes as the government tries to pass a bill to end hereditary peerages ahead of a spring deadline.

item.title: Ugandan students who fled Iran air strikes recall 'ground trembling'
item.description: Some opted to remain in Iran, even after their government offered to bring them home.

item.title: 'Massive' numbers killed by gunmen in latest Nigeria attack, senator tells BBC
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants reportedly seized control of a village for two days before being forced out by troops and air strikes.

item.title: Russia was behind parcel fires in UK and Europe, investigators say
item.description: The attacks, which involved self-igniting packages, targeted couriers in Poland, Germany and at a DHL depot in the UK in 2024.

item.title: Badenoch presses Starmer to strike Iranian missile sites
item.description: RAF jets have been shooting down missiles fired by Iran, but are not taking part in strikes on its sites.

item.title: Labour whip restored to farm tax plan rebel MP
item.description: Markus Campbell-Savours says he is relieved after rebelling against the government's farm tax plan.

item.title: 'We couldn't sleep because of fear': Residents flee as Israel pounds south Beirut
item.description: Communities across huge swathes of Lebanon have been told by Israel to leave their homes due to military action against Hezbollah.

item.title: Israeli troops push into Lebanon for yet another war with Iran's proxy Hezbollah
item.description: The BBC sees troops and tanks massing near the border, as speculation mounts that Israel is planning a full-scale ground invasion to fight Hezbollah.

item.title: Mandelson's bail conditions lifted after misconduct in public office arrest
item.description: Lord Peter Mandelson has had his passport returned by police, his lawyers say.

item.title: Indonesia to ban social media and other online platforms for under 16s
item.description: It is the latest country to curb young people's access to digital spaces to try and protect them from abuse.

item.title: Azerbaijan furious with Iran as war spills over its border
item.description: President Ilham Aliyev says drone strikes are an "act of terror" as he places the armed forces on high alert.

item.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
item.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

item.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy tycoon Jimmy Lai will not appeal conviction
item.description: Lai was convicted and sentenced to 20 years in jail last month on charges for sedition and collusion with foreign forces.

item.title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
item.description: The country is waiting to see if the government seizes on this moment to disarm the Iranian-backed armed group and how the militants will respond.

item.title: Tears and cheers as controversial long-running Australian breakfast radio show implodes
item.description: The end of the Kyle and Jackie O Show, whose hosts had a combined A$200m contract, has shocked the media industry.

item.title: US eases sanctions on Russian oil sales to India during Iran conflict
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent gives India a 30-day waiver to buy Russian crude as a "stop gap measure".

item.title: Jamil Jivani, a Friend of JD Vance, Channels Charlie Kirk in Canada
item.description: Jamil Jivani, a Conservative rookie member of Parliament, follows an American playbook to win over young men on college campuses.

item.title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
item.description: After a long battle with drug addiction, Sae Joon Park felt settled in a new life. But he was deported last year and is now fighting to get back to Hawaii.

item.title: US and Venezuela agree to resume diplomatic ties after Maduro capture
item.description: The US said the two sides would make joint efforts to promote stability after the US seized Maduro and his wife.

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut after evacuation warning sparks panic
item.description: Huge traffic jams form after the unprecedented warning for people to leave the Hezbollah stronghold, as Israel steps up its strikes across Lebanon.

item.title: Sri Lanka takes control of second Iranian vessel a day after US sub attack
item.description: The South Asian country is caught in the middle of the US-Israeli military campaign against Iran.

item.title: The young women who fought in Ethiopia's last civil war and don't want to see another one
item.description: Female fighters recall how the conflict in the northern Tigray destroyed their lives, as fears grow that fighting could resume.

item.title: 'What is the game plan?': The Iran war is unsettling China and its ambitions
item.description: China is not feeling the shock of war in the Middle East - yet. But it is feeling the ripples.

item.title: Finland plans to lift decades-old ban on hosting nuclear weapons
item.description: The government says the nation's defence environment has "significantly changed" since Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Regragui departs as Morocco appoint Ouahbi as new coach
item.description: Morocco part company with coach Walid Regragui four months before the World Cup finals, with Mohamed Ouahbi appointed in his place.

item.title: 'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
item.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

item.title: 'This is war': BBC speaks to Iranians at Iran-Turkey border
item.description: BBC correspondent James Waterhouse speaks to people at the border, after US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

item.title: Checkpoints everywhere and internet blackouts: Life in Iran as US-Israeli strikes intensify
item.description: Iranians are coping with daily strikes and security crackdowns - all while trying to stay in touch with loved ones.

item.title: Labour MP 'voluntarily suspends herself' amid China spy probe
item.description: Joani Reid said she had temporarily stood down from the party after her husband was arrested on suspicion of spying for China.

item.title: PM stands by decision not to join strikes on Iran and sends more jets to Qatar
item.description: The PM said the best way forward was a "negotiated settlement with Iran where they give up their nuclear ambitions".

item.title: Iran's high-risk war strategy seems to centre on endurance and deterrence
item.description: Tehran's approach appears to rest on a belief it can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain pain and costs, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azimi.

item.title: US asked Ukraine for help fighting Iranian drones, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukraine's president says Kyiv will only help if doing so does not deplete its own air defences.

item.title: Caf postpones Wafcon 2026 just 12 days before kick-off
item.description: The 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations is postponed just 12 days before the finals were due to kick off in Morocco.

item.title: Conservative Anglicans pull back from electing rival to Archbishop of Canterbury
item.description: The group had said it would choose a new leader with the same title as Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church.

item.title: Iran school and nearby military base struck multiple times, satellite image reveals
item.description: Iranian authorities say the attack, which took place on Saturday, killed 168 people.

item.title: Ecuadorean troops find 35m-long 'narco-sub' hidden in nature reserve
item.description: Ecuador's military says the drug-trafficking submersible was loaded with fuel and "ready to go".

item.title: MP's husband and two political advisers bailed after arrests over alleged China spying
item.description: One of those arrested is the husband of Scottish Labour MP Joani Reid.

item.title: Failed asylum seeker families to be offered up to £40k to leave UK
item.description: The home secretary says the "incentives" will deliver a "significant saving to the taxpayer".

item.title: Healey visits Cyprus after criticism of UK response to drone attacks
item.description: The Cypriot government has criticised how the UK has handled information sharing after drone attacks.

item.title: Israel and America want the Kurds to join the fight in Iran
item.description: Kurdish militias are weighing a risky bid to redraw their neighbour’s borders

item.title: Senedd election is referendum on Starmer's leadership, says Nigel Farage
item.description: Nigel Farage helps the party launch its Senedd election manifesto, including a 1p income tax cut.

item.title: How the Danes and Swedes handle populism
item.description: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen calls an election to take on the hard right again

item.title: Meet the weekend warriors preparing to defend Europe from Russia
item.description: Lithuania’s citizen-soldiers are part of growing volunteer forces in the Baltics

item.title: Feted by Europe’s left, Spain’s Pedro Sánchez is unloved at home
item.description: The prime minister is trying to become an icon of resistance to Donald Trump

item.title: Why one of Germany’s richest regions is gripped with anxiety
item.description: Baden-Württemberg’s election is overshadowed by worries over deindustrialisation

item.title: In African development, big is beautiful again
item.description: With aid declining, grand visions for growth are back in fashion

item.title: Thousands of Africans are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: African governments are belatedly trying to stop them

item.title: Allegations against a corruption watchdog rock Malaysian politics
item.description: The prime minister, Anwar Ibrahim, repeats his predecessors’ answer

item.title: Welcome to Kashiwazaki, home to the world’s largest nuclear plant
item.description: What one small city says about modern Japan’s development

item.title: Javier Milei aggressively celebrates a string of successes
item.description: Argentina’s president has much to shout about. But job worries loom

item.title: China sets its lowest growth target for a generation
item.description: In macroeconomics, modesty is not always a virtue

item.title: China’s first railway project in the EU is open at last
item.description: Once a show of largesse, it now reflects China’s struggles on the continent

item.title: Nigel Farage and Zack Polanski: best of frenemies
item.description: Britain’s twin populists have a symbiotic relationship

item.title: AI danger gets real
item.description: The squabble between America’s government and Anthropic makes an AI disaster more likely

item.title: Binyamin Netanyahu has his war
item.description: If it goes wrong, he will also get the blame

item.title: Nepalis vote in first election since Gen Z uprising for change
item.description: This election will test whether the country is ready for a new, untested generation to shape its future.

item.title: Ethiopia experiments with 'smart' police stations that have no officers
item.description: A project to introduce unmanned sites is part of a broader adoption of digital technologies.

item.title: Iran targets headquarters of Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq
item.description: The Iranian military's strikes come amid speculation that the US wants Iranian Kurdish groups to join its conflict with Iran.

item.title: Trying to get social care can be 'horrendous', Baroness Casey tells BBC
item.description: The chair of the independent commission on adult social care also says the care system relies on the exploitation of its workforce.

item.title: Reform leads other parties in donations ahead of May elections
item.description: The latest donations received by Nigel Farage's party includes £3m from crypto investor Christopher Harborne.

item.title: Investigative journalism in India is under threat
item.description: Politicians and tycoons are tightening their grip

item.title: Russia’s Starlink shutdown is a blow to its soldiers and drones
item.description: Launching its own satellite network is not realistic

item.title: Donald Trump must stop soon
item.description: His ill-considered conflict risks descending into chaos

item.title: It’s time to unleash Europe’s pensions
item.description: One reform offers both security in old age and dynamism now
